summary: Completed testing of Gen 1 Auto-Binder feature. Backend: 17/17 tests passed after fixing a regex bug. Frontend: Toast message and binder filter working correctly.
passed_tests: Backend: POST /api/cards with Pikachu (EN Gen 1) returns auto_binder='Génération 1'
Backend: POST /api/cards with Charizard (EN Gen 1) returns auto_binder='Génération 1'
Backend: POST /api/cards with Mewtwo (EN Gen 1) returns auto_binder='Génération 1'
Backend: POST /api/cards with Dracaufeu (FR Gen 1) returns auto_binder='Génération 1'
Backend: POST /api/cards with Ectoplasma (FR Gen 1) returns auto_binder='Génération 1'
Backend: POST /api/cards with Ronflex (FR Gen 1) returns auto_binder='Génération 1'
Backend: POST /api/cards with Lucario (Gen 4) does NOT have auto_binder
Backend: POST /api/cards with Greninja (Gen 6) does NOT have auto_binder
Backend: POST /api/cards with Rayquaza (Gen 3) does NOT have auto_binder
Backend: POST /api/cards with 'Pikachu EX' (suffix) still detects Gen 1
Backend: POST /api/cards with 'Dracaufeu VMAX' (suffix) still detects Gen 1
Backend: POST /api/cards with 'Charizard GX' (suffix) still detects Gen 1
Backend: POST /api/cards with 'Mewtwo VSTAR' (suffix) still detects Gen 1
Backend: GET /api/binders shows 'Génération 1' binder after adding Gen 1 card
Backend: Multiple Gen 1 cards use the same 'Génération 1' binder (reuse)
Backend: Duplicate cards increment quantity instead of creating new card
Backend: 'Génération 1' binder has correct French description
Frontend: Login as admin works correctly
Frontend: Add card modal opens and search works
Frontend: Toast shows 'Génération 1' binder assignment after adding Gen 1 card
Frontend: 'Génération 1' binder filter is visible in collection view

bugs_fixed.issue: Regex in is_gen1_pokemon() was incorrectly stripping 'ex' from 'Ronflex' because it matched the suffix pattern
bugs_fixed.fix: Changed regex from '\s*(ex|gx|...)' to '\s+(ex|gx|...)' to require a space before the suffix

seed_data_creation: Test cards created with TEST_ prefix in card_name for cleanup
context_for_next_testing_agent: Gen 1 Auto-Binder feature is fully working. When a Gen 1 Pokemon card (EN or FR name) is added, it's automatically assigned to the 'Génération 1' binder. The binder is auto-created if it doesn't exist. Non-Gen 1 Pokemon are NOT auto-assigned. Suffixes like EX, VMAX, GX, VSTAR are properly stripped before checking. A bug was fixed where 'Ronflex' (Snorlax in French) was incorrectly not detected as Gen 1 because the regex was stripping 'ex' from the end.

features_verified.gen1_english_detection: Pikachu, Charizard, Mewtwo correctly detected as Gen 1
features_verified.gen1_french_detection: Dracaufeu, Ectoplasma, Ronflex correctly detected as Gen 1
features_verified.non_gen1_exclusion: Lucario, Greninja, Rayquaza correctly NOT assigned to Gen 1 binder
features_verified.suffix_handling: Pikachu EX, Dracaufeu VMAX, Charizard GX, Mewtwo VSTAR all correctly detected
features_verified.binder_auto_creation: 'Génération 1' binder is auto-created if it doesn't exist
features_verified.binder_reuse: Multiple Gen 1 cards use the same binder
features_verified.duplicate_handling: Duplicate cards increment quantity instead of creating new card
features_verified.frontend_toast: Toast shows 'Carte ajoutée au classeur Génération 1' when adding Gen 1 card
features_verified.frontend_filter: 'Génération 1' binder filter visible in collection view

rca_of_the_issue.issue: Ronflex (French name for Snorlax) was not being detected as Gen 1
rca_of_the_issue.root_cause: The regex in is_gen1_pokemon() function was using '\s*' (zero or more spaces) before the suffix pattern, which caused 'ex' at the end of 'Ronflex' to be matched and stripped, leaving 'ronfl' which is not in the Gen 1 set
reproduction_steps: 2. Regex strips 'ex' from the end, leaving 'ronfl'
rca_of_the_issue.fix_applied: Changed regex from '\s*(ex|gx|vmax|vstar|v|tag team|break|lv\.x|prime)\s*$' to '\s+(ex|gx|vmax|vstar|v|tag team|break|lv\.x|prime)\s*$' - requiring at least one space before the suffix
rca_of_the_issue.verification: All 17 backend tests now pass, including Ronflex detection
